questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra War did Drona fall?
questions.explanation: Drona was slain on the fifteenth day, the last of his five days as commander.

questions.q: What false news broke Drona's will to fight?
options: That his son Ashwatthama was dead
questions.explanation: The cry 'Ashwatthama is dead' made Drona lower his weapons in grief.

questions.q: Who actually died bearing the name Ashwatthama that day?
questions.explanation: Bhima slew an elephant named Ashwatthama so the claim would carry a shadow of truth.

questions.q: Who killed the elephant to set the stratagem in motion?
questions.explanation: Bhima killed the war elephant Ashwatthama and roared the news toward Drona.

questions.q: Whose confirmation did Drona demand before believing his son was dead?
questions.explanation: Drona trusted only Yudhishthira, famed never to lie even for the world's wealth.

questions.q: Who beheaded the unarmed, meditating Drona?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna, born to destroy Drona, cut off his head as he sat weaponless.

questions.q: What was Drona's role in the Kaurava army when he died?
questions.explanation: Drona had led the Kaurava forces since Bhishma's fall on day ten.

questions.q: What did Drona do upon hearing Yudhishthira's reply?
options: He laid down his weapons and sat in meditation
He charged at Bhima in rage
He called for his chariot to retreat
questions.explanation: Grief-stricken, Drona abandoned his arms and entered yoga on the chariot floor.

questions.q: Whose idea was the false-death stratagem against Drona?
questions.explanation: Krishna counseled that only the news of Ashwatthama's death could stop the unstoppable teacher.

questions.q: Dhrishtadyumna, Drona's slayer, was the son of which king?
questions.explanation: He was the fire-born son of Drupada, king of Panchala and Drona's bitter rival.

questions.q: What exactly did Yudhishthira say about Ashwatthama?
options: A plain lie that the man was slain
'Ashwatthama is dead' — adding indistinctly, 'man or elephant'
That he had seen the body himself
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira said 'Ashwatthama hatah' aloud and added 'naro va kunjaro va' (man or elephant) indistinctly; in popular retellings, drums and conches drown out the qualifier.

questions.q: What legendary consequence did Yudhishthira's half-truth carry?
options: His tongue burned for a day
Draupadi refused to speak to him
He lost the war's first salute
His chariot, which floated above the earth, sank to the ground
questions.explanation: The epic says Yudhishthira's chariot had hovered for his truthfulness — after the equivocation it touched the earth.

questions.q: Why was Dhrishtadyumna fated to kill Drona?
options: He was born from a sacrifice Drupada performed for a son to slay Drona
Shiva's boon marked Drona as his prey
A dying sage cursed Drona through him
questions.explanation: Drupada's yajna yielded Dhrishtadyumna, born armored from the flames for this single destiny.

questions.q: What old grievance drove Drupada's hatred of Drona?
options: Drona rejected his daughter's hand for Ashwatthama
Defeated by Drona's students, he was spared but stripped of half his kingdom
Drona had him exiled from the Kuru court
questions.explanation: Drona used the Pandavas to seize northern Panchala as fee for an old insult, leaving Drupada burning for revenge.

questions.q: Which Pandava loudly objected to the beheading of the disarmed teacher?
questions.explanation: Arjuna cried out to take Drona alive, horrified at the slaying of their unarmed guru.

questions.q: What did witnesses report of Drona's final moment, as the epic tells it?
options: A rain of flowers hid his corpse
His bow rose and fought on alone
His spirit visibly ascended like a light to heaven
questions.explanation: The epic says only five — Krishna, Arjuna, Kripa, Yudhishthira, and Sanjaya — saw Drona's spirit blaze up to Brahmaloka before the blade ever fell.

questions.q: Which two kings had Drona slain earlier that same day, marking his terrible final rampage?
questions.explanation: Drona killed both old Drupada and Virata on day fifteen, sharpening Dhrishtadyumna's fury.

questions.q: What criticism had the sages leveled at Drona just before his death?
options: That he served a usurper for gold
That he had abandoned his son
That he used celestial weapons against ordinary soldiers, against dharma
questions.explanation: Celestial rishis appeared and rebuked Drona for burning common troops with divine astras, telling him his hour had come.

questions.q: How did Bhima goad Drona as the stratagem unfolded?
options: He taunted that a brahmin who kills for a living betrays his own dharma
He dragged Ashwatthama's banner through the mud
He challenged Drona to a mace duel
He mocked Drona's poverty of old
questions.explanation: Bhima's barb — a brahmin slaughtering kshatriyas for a king's pay — deepened Drona's despair at his own life.

questions.q: How long did Drona command the Kaurava army in total?
questions.explanation: Drona led from day eleven through day fifteen, between Bhishma's fall and his own.

questions.q: What had Drona promised Duryodhana but repeatedly failed to deliver during his command?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana wanted Yudhishthira taken alive to force a new dice game; Arjuna's defense foiled every attempt.

questions.q: Who had earlier revealed to the Pandavas that Drona was unconquerable while he held his weapons?
options: Bhishma, from his bed of arrows
questions.explanation: Drona had admitted he could be slain only when, struck by unbearable grief, he laid his weapons down.

questions.q: What weapon had Drona unleashed on day fifteen that the rishis condemned?
questions.explanation: Drona loosed Brahma's weapon on rank-and-file warriors, a use the celestial seers called adharma.

questions.q: What did Ashwatthama unleash in fury when he learned how his father died?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama hurled the Narayanastra, quenched only when Krishna had the army dismount and surrender to it.

questions.q: How was the Narayanastra that avenged Drona neutralized?
options: All warriors laid down arms and bowed before it, on Krishna's order
It was drawn into the Ganga
questions.explanation: The astra spares the unresisting; Krishna's command to disarm and dismount let it pass harmlessly.

questions.q: Which warrior of the Pandava camp furiously charged at Dhrishtadyumna for slaying the unarmed Drona?
questions.explanation: Satyaki, outraged at the slaying of the unarmed guru, seized a mace and rushed at Dhrishtadyumna; Bhima had to hold him back as the two traded bitter insults.

questions.q: What rank-and-status irony shadowed Drona's whole career, culminating at his death?
options: A poor man who refused all wealth
A brahmin living the warrior's life, dying a warrior's death
questions.explanation: Born to priesthood, Drona lived by the bow — Bhima's taunt and the rishis' rebuke both turned on this.

questions.q: Per the epic's accounting, who succeeded Drona as Kaurava commander?
questions.explanation: Karna took command on day sixteen, with Shalya and then briefly Ashwatthama following.

questions.q: Yudhishthira's equivocation is often cited in dharma debates because Krishna justified it how?
options: As a king's privilege in war
As no lie at all, since an Ashwatthama had died
As a lesser sin accepted to stop greater slaughter
As Yudhishthira's own karma to bear alone
questions.explanation: Krishna argued that protecting the world from Drona's astras outweighed the stain of a bent truth.

questions.q: What detail about Drona's age makes his last stand the more formidable?
options: He was eighty-five years old, by the epic's reckoning
He had fasted through the war
He fought with a broken arm from day twelve
questions.explanation: The Drona Parva says that at eighty-five Drona careered through battle like a youth of sixteen, still nearly invincible with bow in hand.
